2604.08333.title: Lost in the Hype: Revealing and Dissecting the Performance Degradation of Medical Multimodal Large Language Models in Image Classification
2604.08333.paper_summary: The rise of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) has sparked an unprecedented wave of applications in the field of medical imaging analysis. However, as one of the earliest and most fundamental tasks integrated into this paradigm, medical image classification reveals a sobering reality: state-of-the-art medical MLLMs consistently underperform compared to traditional deep learning models, despite their overwhelming advantages in pre-training data and model parameters. This paradox prompts a critical rethinking: where exactly does the performance degradation originate? In this paper, we conduct extensive experiments on 14 open-source medical MLLMs across three representative image classification datasets. Moving beyond superficial performance benchmarking, we employ feature probing to track the information flow of visual features module-by-module and layer-by-layer throughout the entire MLLM pipeline, enabling explicit visualization of where and how classification signals are distorted, diluted, or overridden. As the first attempt to dissect classification performance degradation in medical MLLMs, our findings reveal four failure modes: 1) quality limitation in visual representation, 2) fidelity loss in connector projection, 3) comprehension deficit in LLM reasoning, and 4) misalignment of semantic mapping. Meanwhile, we introduce quantitative scores that characterize the healthiness of feature evolution, enabling principled comparisons across diverse MLLMs and datasets. Furthermore, we provide insightful discussions centered on the critical barriers that prevent current medical MLLMs from fulfilling their promised clinical potential. We hope that our work provokes rethinking within the community-highlighting that the road from high expectations to clinically deployable MLLMs remains long and winding.
2604.08333.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）的興起在醫學影像分析領域引發了前所未有的應用浪潮。<lb/>然而，作為這一範式中最早且最基本的任務之一，醫學影像分類揭示了一個令人清醒的現實：儘管在預訓練數據和模型參數上具有壓倒性的優勢，最先進的醫學MLLMs在性能上仍然不及傳統深度學習模型。<lb/>這一悖論促使我們進行重要的重新思考：性能下降究竟源於何處？<lb/>在本文中，我們對14個開源醫學MLLMs在三個具有代表性的影像分類數據集上進行了廣泛的實驗。<lb/>超越表面的性能基準，我們採用特徵探測方法，逐模塊和逐層追蹤視覺特徵的信息流，從而明確可視化分類信號在何處以及如何被扭曲、稀釋或覆蓋。<lb/>作為首次試圖剖析醫學MLLMs中分類性能下降的研究，我們的發現揭示了四種失敗模式：1）視覺表徵的質量限制，2）連接器投影中的保真度損失，3）LLM推理中的理解缺陷，以及4）語義映射的不對齊。<lb/>同時，我們引入了量化分數來表徵特徵演變的健康性，從而實現不同MLLMs和數據集之間的原則性比較。<lb/>此外，我們提供了有見地的討論，集中於當前醫學MLLMs無法實現其承諾的臨床潛力的關鍵障礙。<lb/>我們希望我們的工作能夠引發社群的重新思考，強調從高期望到可臨床部署的MLLMs之路仍然漫長而曲折。
2604.08333.author: Xun Zhu et.al.
2604.08333.authors: Xun Zhu, Fanbin Mo, Xi Chen, Kaili Zheng, Shaoshuai Yang, Yiming Shi, Jian Gao, Miao Li, Ji Wu
